title: I got Diagnosed with Autism & some Persecuted Personality Disorder

enrichment.summary: Kyle discusses his dual diagnosis of Asperger's autism and persecuted personality disorder, obtained during a psychological evaluation after a DUI. He reads through his neuropsychological test results and the evaluating psychiatrist's written report, commenting on each finding and sharing personal anecdotes about how the diagnoses reflect his actual experiences with attention, memory, social interaction, sensory sensitivities, and school.
enrichment.speaker: mixed

stances.position: Disagrees it reflects paranoia; says his concerns about people disliking him are warranted, not pathological
Accepts it and sought it out; sees it as explaining lifelong social and sensory differences
Has always disliked taking psychiatric medications and mostly refused them
Strongly negative; believes lecture-based instruction failed him and wasted his time, particularly in math
Strongly prefers audiobooks; believes repeated listening is a superior learning method for him
Smokes daily and frames it neutrally as part of his routine; disclosed it honestly during evaluation
Views them as fans rather than threats, unlike how his mother characterizes his situation

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He had to delete a previous video because he talked about someone else and got reprimanded.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He was diagnosed with autism (Asperger's) and persecuted personality disorder.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He sought a psychological evaluation after a DUI to use the autism diagnosis in court.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He believes smoking weed can help manage autism symptoms.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The psychological evaluation consisted of talking to a psychiatrist, taking a test with pictures and questions, and a computer multiple-choice test.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He scored high on the persecuted personality disorder test, answering 'yes' to questions about visions and people being out to get him.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He was kicked out of a university education program, which he discussed in a YouTube video.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He doesn't consider himself paranoid like typical persecuted personality disorder cases, despite the diagnosis.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He smokes weed frequently, including before the evaluation.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He scored low average on list learning and list recall tests but high average on story memory.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He excelled in visuospatial construction and figure recall tests, scoring 'superior.'

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He struggled with semantic fluency and picture naming tests.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has difficulty with verbal memory tasks but good long-term memory.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He performed poorly on the trail-making test due to rushing and making errors.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He believes the 20-questions test was flawed because he wasn't given enough time to think.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He scored high on the paranoia scale in the personality questionnaire but attributes it to feeling mistreated.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has a history of social interaction difficulties, few friends, and imaginative play as a child.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Teachers suspected hearing issues because he often didn't respond to verbal instructions.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has a loud, unmodulated voice and struggles with giving concise stories.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He follows strict routines, like eating the same meals and watching specific YouTube channels.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has hypersensitivity to temperature, water, wool, and skin-to-skin contact while sleeping.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He was diagnosed with ADHD as a child but dislikes taking medications.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He disagrees with past diagnoses of anxiety and bipolar disorder.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has a history of depression in adolescence due to school problems.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He used to binge drink but quit years ago and now smokes weed daily.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has a retinal tear and occasional leg jerks at night.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: His family has a history of Alzheimer's, alcohol abuse, and mental health issues.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He was placed in remedial classes in third and seventh grades due to academic struggles.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He earned a bachelor's degree in English but was removed from an education program for political reasons and poor grades.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He taught English abroad and was married for three years before divorcing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He works as a window washer and spends hours daily on his website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He prefers auditory learning over reading due to slow reading speed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He believes listening to audiobooks is a superior learning method compared to reading.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He criticizes traditional education, especially math classes, for being ineffective for his learning style.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He resents teachers and authority figures, expressing anger toward them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: His mother often sent him to mental health professionals as a child, which he disliked.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He denies having psychosis but acknowledges concerns about people deceiving him.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: His mother believes his paranoia about online trolls is justified, but he considers them fans.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He had a staph infection that turned into cancer and required surgery.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He dislikes taking medications, including antidepressants and ADHD drugs.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He was placed in remedial classes because teachers misinterpreted his boredom as disinterest or lack of intelligence.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He believes his imaginative and deep thinking made traditional schooling frustrating and ineffective for him.
